MetaD Protein + RNA

Current results for the MetaD experiment that contains both Protein and correct RNA target in the system.

Brady Johnston

Report Auto-generated: 17 July, 2020

This report has been auto-generated. If all goes well, the latest COLVAR_METAD and HILLS files should be downloaded from Topaz and then the numbers crunched.

The working files were last edited at 2020-07-17 07:53:06.

Report Results:

Read in COLVAR file

run <- "./"
metadata <- "Protein + RNA"
fl <- list.files(run, pattern = "COLVAR", recursive = T, full.names = T)
df <- read.table(fl)
colnames(df) <- c("time", "path.sss", "path.zzz", "metad.rbias", "walls.bias", "rmsd0", "rmsd1")
head(df)
##   time path.sss  path.zzz metad.rbias walls.bias    rmsd0    rmsd1
## 1    0 9.707039 -0.028058    0.000000          0 0.043497 2.123624
## 2    1 9.712674 -0.024705    0.000000          0 0.068640 2.124752
## 3    2 9.715892 -0.023344    0.999771          0 0.076695 2.129205
## 4    3 9.707418 -0.023401    1.970424          0 0.082567 2.122518
## 5    4 9.707432 -0.022187    2.913710          0 0.090124 2.124700
## 6    5 9.704295 -0.022889    3.831537          0 0.090311 2.126099

Initial Z vs S plot.

zvs <- ggplot(df, aes(path.sss, path.zzz, colour = time / 1e3)) + 
  # geom_density2d() +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 0.3) +
  scale_colour_viridis_c() + 
  simple_light() + 
  scale_x_continuous(breaks = 1:10, limits = c(1,10)) +
  labs(title = "Z vs S: MetaD, lambda = 10", colour = "Time (ns)", 
       subtitle = metadata)
  # ggsave(paste(run,"z_vs_s.pdf", sep = "_"), width = 5, height = 3)
zvs

Density plot

ggplot(df, aes(path.sss, path.zzz)) + 
  geom_density_2d_filled(contour_var = "ndensity" ) + 
  # scale_fill_brewer() +
  simple_light() + 
  scale_x_continuous(limits = c(1,10), breaks = 1:10, expand = c(0,0)) + 
  scale_y_continuous(limits = c(0,2.5), expand = c(0,0)) + 
  theme(panel.grid = element_blank()) + 
  labs(title = "Z vs S Density", 
       subtitle = metadata, 
       fill = "Relative Density")
## Warning: Removed 6640 rows containing non-finite values (stat_density2d_filled).

Reading in Heights

df2 <- read.table(list.files(path = run, pattern = "HILLS", recursive = T, full = T))

colnames(df2) <- c("time", "path.sss", "path.zzz", "sigma_path.sss", "sigma_path.zzz", "height", "biasf")
head(df2)
##   time path.sss    path.zzz sigma_path.sss sigma_path.zzz    height biasf
## 1    1 9.712674 -0.02470489            0.3           0.08 1.0714286    15
## 2    2 9.715892 -0.02334434            0.3           0.08 1.0404769    15
## 3    3 9.707418 -0.02340117            0.3           0.08 1.0112824    15
## 4    4 9.707432 -0.02218700            0.3           0.08 0.9836960    15
## 5    5 9.704295 -0.02288914            0.3           0.08 0.9575767    15
## 6    6 9.696488 -0.02249420            0.3           0.08 0.9329187    15

Plotting height over time.

ggplot(df2, aes(time/1e3, height)) + 
  geom_line() + 
  scale_y_continuous() + 
  simple_light() + 
  labs(x = "Time (ns)", 
       y = "Height",
       title = "Height over Time", 
       subtitle = metadata)

  # ggsave(paste(run,"height.pdf", sep = "_"), width = 5, height = 3)

RMSD Plotting

df %>% 
  select(time, rmsd0, rmsd1) %>% 
  pivot_longer(cols = c(rmsd0, rmsd1)) %>% 
 
ggplot(aes(time/1e3, y = value, colour = name)) + #, colour = time, group = name)) +
  # scale_colour_viridis_c() +
  # facet_wrap(~name, ncol = 1) + 
  # theme(aspect.ratio = 1/5) +
  geom_line() +
  simple_light() + 
  scale_x_continuous() +
  labs(title = "RMSD over Time", 
       subtitle = metadata,
       x = "Time (ns)", 
       y = "RMSD", 
       colour = "Model")

  # ggsave(paste(run,"RMSD.pdf", sep = "_"), width = 5, height = 3)
ggplot(df, aes(time/1e3, metad.rbias)) + 
  # geom_point(alpha = 0.3, size = 0.3) + 
  geom_line() +
  scale_colour_viridis_c() + 
  scale_y_continuous() +
  simple_light() + 
  labs(title = "rbias: MetaD, lambda = 10", 
       subtitle = metadata,
       colour = "Time (ns)", 
       x = "Time (ns)")

  # ggsave(paste(run,".pdf", sep = "_"), width = 5, height = 3)
